title: SF Debris Reviews Evangelion 222 and Actually Likes It
display_title: SF Debris Reviews Evangelion 222 and Actually Likes It
wikitext:
[]: SF Debris Reviews Evangelion 2.22, and Actually Likes It.             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Tue Sep 18, 2012 6:20 pm Site link: http://sfdebris.com/videos/animation/evangelion2.asp Blip TV Links Part 1: http://blip.tv/sf-debris-opinionated-reviews/evangelion-2-22-review-part-1-6359614 Part 2: http://blip.tv/sf-debris-opinionated-reviews/evangelion-2-22-review-part-2-6359642 PArt 3: http://blip.tv/sf-debris-opinionated-reviews/evangelion-2-22-review-part-3-6359656 YouTube Preview/Comment Link: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FQep10KVluE Haven't watched it all yet, hence the lack of subtitle, but here's the links. :)

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Wed Sep 19, 2012 2:31 am http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13066/SF-Debris-reviews-Evangelion-111-and-gets-completely-lost/

[]:             Quote Post by beecass » Wed Sep 19, 2012 3:20 am I was a big fan of the 1.11 review, as it really hit the nail on the head in terms of the film's accessibility to an audience unfamiliar with Evangelion - I'll be very interested to see if this review is similarly apt.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Wed Sep 19, 2012 10:28 am Imho a very bad review.... 99% is just a retelling the whole story with spoilers and only mentioning that all characters are mentally ill and that naked kids are annoying. Just 2 minutes before the whole thing ends, he starts to say what is good and bad about the movie. Lame.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Sep 19, 2012 1:44 pm ^ Well, as a review it really is bad, but as a funny little deconstruction about ridiculous things an eva-fan would never notice, it is great!

[]:             Quote Post by 0Megabyte » Wed Sep 19, 2012 2:12 pm Imho a very bad review.... 99% is just a retelling the whole story with spoilers and only mentioning that all characters are mentally ill and that naked kids are annoying. Just 2 minutes before the whole thing ends, he starts to say what is good and bad about the movie. Lame.

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Thu Sep 20, 2012 9:00 am I found the review hilarious. Though I was surprised that he actually liked the movie. Perhaps his position will change after having seen the series. If nothing else the movie got him interested in that.

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Thu Sep 20, 2012 9:58 am He said people's whining comments about the ending were discouraging him. I've been following him for years, but I've never sent him an email before. That remark moved me to action, so I gave him an alternate perspective on the matter (ie. both endings are brilliant).

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Thu Sep 20, 2012 4:35 pm 0Megabyte wrote: Well, actually, his style of review isn't the sort you'd see on Roger Ebert's website. It's a show , and he goes into a lot of detail. Watch his other reviews and you'll see the same thing. It's perfectly alright if you don't like his style. But it is a style , not shitty reviewing.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Thu Sep 20, 2012 11:15 pm qu4d wrote: Imho a very bad review.... 99% is just a retelling the whole story with spoilers and only mentioning that all characters are mentally ill and that naked kids are annoying.

[]: Prude? Hell no. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Sep 21, 2012 1:03 am With this same message (and a donation) I've also finally convinced him to review... something . EDIT: Crap, I accidentally deleted the rest of this...

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Fri Sep 21, 2012 1:50 am "Storyline"... Red Letter Media owes an apology to humanity for what it's done.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Fri Sep 21, 2012 2:39 am lol why? i think it's funny as hell and their reviews are still very very good and well reasoned.

[]: Re: Prude? Hell no. B/             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Fri Sep 21, 2012 3:20 am Alaska Slim wrote: With this same message (and a donation) I've also finally convinced him to review... something .

[]: Re: Prude? Hell no. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Sep 21, 2012 3:38 am BornIn1142 wrote: Before the 1.11 and 2.22 announcement, I was actually sort of considering the idea of requesting a review of EoE. Since that would also require viewing the whole series as well though, I dismissed it as a stupid, expensive, stupid idea.

[]:             Quote Post by 0Megabyte » Sun Sep 23, 2012 1:49 am qu4d wrote: But his main focus is the "bad" stuff of the movie and this for ~40 minutes? It became so boring after a few minutes. And there are effin great review-shoes like half in the bag, where they even have a storyline... http://redlettermedia.com/half-in-the-bag/ http://www.redlettermedia.com/half-in-the-bag/jack-and-jill http://redlettermedia.com/half-in-the-bag/special-edition-the-zookeeper-trailer/ http://redlettermedia.com/plinkett/star-wars/star-wars-episode-1-the-phantom-menace/ http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-x1YuvUQFJ0 <--- making fun of a movie they even thought was ok

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Sep 23, 2012 4:36 pm Funny review is funny. Im not in the habit of getting in a tiss because he isnt majorly clued up on the show or anime generally. And he does spot some stuff I didnt see on the first few viewings. He can riff on it as much as he likes, as long as the riffs are good.

[]: Re: Prude? Hell no. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Feb 06, 2014 10:40 am Alaska Slim wrote: With this same message (and a donation) I've also finally convinced him to review... something .

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Thu Feb 06, 2014 8:14 pm Man, I can't wait for his reaction to Q when he gets around to it.
summary: []: SF Debris Reviews Evangelion 2.22, and Actually Likes It.             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Tue Sep 18, 2012 6:20 pm Site link: http://sfdebris.com/videos/animation/evangelion2.asp Blip TV Links Part 1: http://blip.tv/sf-debris-opinionated-reviews/evangelion-2-22-review-part-1-6359614 Part 2: http://blip.tv/sf-debris-opinionated-reviews/evangelion-2-22-review-part-2-6359642 PArt 3: http://blip.tv/sf-debris-opinionated-revi
